summary: eth-wallet-sentinel ships wallet/system scanner with hardcoded GitHub Pages C2
details: Package name advertises an Ethereum wallet sentinel utility; tarball contains scanner.js, wallet-scanner.js, and build-verifier.js that together implement a system-information and wallet-credential harvester with a hardcoded exfiltration endpoint. scanner.js loads os/fs/https/child_process, calls os.hostname(), runs `whoami` and other shell commands via execSync, and POSTs the collected data via https.request to https://ddjidd564.github.io (referenced at scanner.js:161 and:323) — a GitHub Pages-hosted host unrelated to any legitimate wallet-monitoring publisher. wallet-scanner.js performs additional curl/ping shell-outs and multiple POST calls. build-verifier.js calls `docker push` via execSync and POSTs hostname/username/cwd over https. The combination of (a) a hardcoded non-publisher C2 reachable from import-time / require-time code paths, (b) collection of host identifiers (hostname, username, cwd) and shell-command output, and (c) wallet-scanner code paths in a package that advertises wallet sentinel functionality, matches the credential-stealer + C2 fingerprint. Installing or loading this package gives the author host fingerprints and any wallet-related material the scanner enumerates.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
